controls.title: Cyber Resilience Framework
controls.description: Establish and maintain a robust CRF incorporating the five IPDRR phases: Identify, Protect, Detect, Respond, and Recover.

maturity.basic: CRF documented covering all 5 phases with defined scope and applicability.
maturity.mature: CRF regularly reviewed and updated based on threat landscape changes; integrated with enterprise risk management framework; mapped to regulatory requirements.
maturity.advanced: CRF continuously improved through automated threat intelligence feeds; benchmarked against international standards (NIST CSF, ISO 27001); adaptive framework with real-time risk scoring.

controls.title: Dedicated Cyber Risk Function
controls.description: Establish a dedicated cyber risk management function with adequate resourcing, skills, and authority to manage cyber threats.

maturity.basic: Dedicated cyber risk function established with defined roles, responsibilities, and reporting lines.
maturity.mature: Function staffed with certified professionals (CISSP, CISM, CRISC); clear escalation procedures; regular skills gap assessments conducted.
maturity.advanced: Function operates with advanced capabilities including threat modelling, red team operations, and strategic threat advisory; retention and succession planning in place.

controls.title: CISO Reporting and Authority
controls.description: Appoint a CISO with direct reporting line to senior management and board, with authority to influence cyber risk decisions across the organisation.

maturity.basic: CISO appointed with documented reporting line to senior management; regular board reporting schedule established.
maturity.mature: CISO participates in executive leadership discussions; provides quarterly board briefings with risk dashboards; has authority to escalate and halt risky initiatives.
maturity.advanced: CISO has strategic influence on business decisions; real-time risk reporting to board via dashboards; independent assurance function validates CISO assessments.

controls.title: Cyber Risk Identification and Assessment
controls.description: Maintain processes for systematic identification, assessment, and prioritisation of cyber risks aligned with the institution's risk appetite.

maturity.basic: Annual cyber risk assessment conducted; risk register maintained with identified threats and vulnerabilities.
maturity.mature: Semi-annual risk assessments with scenario analysis; risk appetite statements specific to cyber; integration with business impact analysis.
maturity.advanced: Continuous risk assessment using automated tools and threat intelligence; quantitative risk modelling (FAIR methodology); dynamic risk appetite adjusted based on threat environment.

controls.title: Threat Escalation Procedures
controls.description: Define and implement clear escalation procedures for cyber threats, ensuring timely communication to senior management and board as warranted.

maturity.basic: Escalation matrix documented with defined severity levels and corresponding notification timelines.
maturity.mature: Automated alerting for critical threats; escalation procedures tested quarterly; integration with incident response workflows.
maturity.advanced: AI-assisted threat classification and automated escalation; real-time threat briefings to leadership; cross-functional war room activation protocols.

controls.title: Board Cyber Risk Oversight
controls.description: Ensure the board maintains adequate oversight of cyber resilience posture through regular reporting, independent assessments, and strategic direction setting.

maturity.basic: Board receives annual cyber risk reports; cyber resilience included in board risk committee agenda.
maturity.mature: Board receives quarterly detailed cyber risk dashboards; independent cyber risk assessments presented annually; board members have baseline cyber literacy.
maturity.advanced: Board leverages real-time cyber risk dashboards; engages external cyber advisors; conducts tabletop exercises at board level; sets measurable cyber risk KPIs.

controls.title: NCII Designation Acknowledgement
controls.description: Acknowledge and document NCII designation status; maintain records of all systems and assets classified as National Critical Information Infrastructure.

maturity.basic: NCII designation documented; inventory of NCII assets maintained with classification details.
maturity.mature: NCII asset inventory regularly validated; dependencies and interconnections mapped; dedicated protection measures applied to all NCII assets.
maturity.advanced: Automated NCII asset discovery and classification; continuous compliance monitoring against NACSA requirements; proactive engagement with NACSA on evolving threats.

controls.title: NACSA Compliance
controls.description: Comply with all NACSA directives, guidelines, and standards applicable to NCII-designated entities, including reporting and audit requirements.

maturity.basic: NACSA compliance requirements documented and tracked; assigned compliance officer for NCII matters.
maturity.mature: Regular self-assessments against NACSA directives; remediation plans tracked to completion; proactive engagement with NACSA during consultations.
maturity.advanced: Integrated compliance management platform tracking NACSA requirements alongside other regulatory obligations; automated compliance evidence collection; participation in NACSA working groups.

controls.title: Additional NCII Security Directives
controls.description: Implement additional security measures as directed by NACSA or BNM for NCII protection, including enhanced monitoring and incident reporting.

maturity.basic: Process to receive, acknowledge, and implement additional NACSA/BNM directives established.
maturity.mature: Dedicated team for rapid implementation of new directives; impact assessment conducted within 48 hours of directive receipt; tracking dashboard maintained.
maturity.advanced: Proactive threat-informed security posture exceeding baseline directives; automated compliance verification for new directives; contributes intelligence back to NACSA.

controls.title: Network Zone Segmentation
controls.description: Implement network segmentation with clearly defined security zones including DMZ, internal, restricted, and management networks with appropriate access controls between zones.

maturity.basic: Network segmented into defined zones with documented architecture diagram; basic ACLs between zones.
maturity.mature: Micro-segmentation implemented for critical assets; zone-based firewall policies regularly reviewed; automated compliance checks for segmentation rules.
maturity.advanced: Software-defined segmentation with zero-trust architecture; dynamic policy enforcement based on identity and context; continuous validation through breach simulation.

controls.title: Server-to-Server Communication Security
controls.description: Secure all server-to-server communications using encrypted channels, mutual authentication, and monitored data flows within and across network zones.

maturity.basic: TLS/mTLS implemented for server-to-server communications; encrypted channels for inter-zone traffic.
maturity.mature: Certificate management automated; traffic flow monitoring between servers; anomalous communication patterns detected and alerted.
maturity.advanced: Service mesh with automated mTLS; real-time traffic analysis with ML-based anomaly detection; automated certificate rotation with zero downtime.

controls.title: Secure Remote Access
controls.description: Implement secure remote access controls including VPN, multi-factor authentication, session management, and endpoint compliance verification.

maturity.basic: VPN with MFA deployed for all remote access; session timeout policies enforced; remote access policy documented.
maturity.mature: Network Access Control (NAC) validates endpoint compliance before granting access; split tunnelling prohibited; privileged remote access through PAM solution.
maturity.advanced: Zero-trust network access (ZTNA) replacing traditional VPN; continuous endpoint posture assessment; adaptive access controls based on risk scoring.

controls.title: Firewall Deployment at Critical Segments
controls.description: Deploy next-generation firewalls at all critical network segments with properly configured rule sets, logging, and regular review cycles.

maturity.basic: Firewalls deployed at perimeter and between critical network segments; rule base documented; annual rule review conducted.
maturity.mature: Next-generation firewalls with application awareness and IPS integration; quarterly rule review with unused rule cleanup; centralised firewall management.
maturity.advanced: AI-driven policy optimisation; automated rule lifecycle management; real-time threat feed integration; firewall-as-code with version-controlled policies.

controls.title: Intrusion Prevention System Deployment
controls.description: Deploy IPS at strategic network points to detect and block known attack signatures and anomalous traffic patterns.

maturity.basic: IPS deployed at perimeter and critical segments; signature database updated regularly; alerts monitored during business hours.
maturity.mature: IPS with custom signatures for institution-specific threats; 24x7 monitoring; tuned to minimise false positives; integrated with SIEM.
maturity.advanced: ML-augmented IPS with behavioural analysis; automated threat response and blocking; virtual patching capability; correlated with threat intelligence.

controls.title: Web and Email Filtering
controls.description: Implement web proxy and email gateway filtering to block malicious content, phishing attempts, and unauthorised data exfiltration.

maturity.basic: Web proxy deployed blocking known malicious categories; email gateway with anti-spam, anti-malware, and attachment filtering.
maturity.mature: SSL/TLS inspection for encrypted web traffic; URL sandboxing; advanced email threat protection with link rewriting and attachment detonation.
maturity.advanced: AI-powered content analysis; real-time threat intelligence integration; automated phishing simulation results feeding filter policies; DMARC/DKIM/SPF enforced.

controls.title: Endpoint Protection
controls.description: Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions on all endpoints with centralised management, real-time monitoring, and automated response capabilities.

maturity.basic: Antivirus/anti-malware deployed on all endpoints with centralised management; signature updates automated; daily scans scheduled.
maturity.mature: EDR solution deployed with behavioural analysis; automated containment of detected threats; endpoint inventory maintained with compliance status.
maturity.advanced: XDR platform correlating endpoint, network, and cloud telemetry; automated investigation and response playbooks; fileless malware detection; threat hunting from endpoints.

controls.title: Full Disk Encryption
controls.description: Implement full disk encryption on all endpoint devices including laptops, desktops, and removable media to prevent data exposure from physical theft or loss.

maturity.basic: Full disk encryption deployed on all laptops and portable devices; encryption keys managed centrally.
maturity.mature: Encryption extended to all workstations and removable media; compliance monitoring automated; key escrow and recovery procedures tested.
maturity.advanced: Hardware-based encryption with TPM integration; automated compliance enforcement preventing unencrypted device usage; encryption status integrated into asset management.

controls.title: Advanced Persistent Threat Solution
controls.description: Deploy APT detection and prevention solutions including network sandboxing, advanced malware analysis, and lateral movement detection.

maturity.basic: Network sandbox solution deployed for file and URL detonation; integration with email and web gateways.
maturity.mature: Multi-vector APT protection covering network, email, and endpoint; threat intelligence correlation; lateral movement detection capabilities.
maturity.advanced: AI-driven APT detection with custom YARA rules and threat actor profiling; deception technology (honeypots/honeynets); automated threat hunting campaigns.

controls.title: Network Packet Capture and NTP Synchronisation
controls.description: Implement full packet capture capability at critical network points and ensure NTP synchronisation across all devices for forensic readiness.

maturity.basic: Packet capture capability at perimeter; NTP synchronisation configured on all network devices and servers.
maturity.mature: Strategic packet capture points at critical segments with adequate retention; NTP hierarchy with redundant stratum sources; metadata indexing for rapid search.
maturity.advanced: Full packet capture with automated analysis and anomaly detection; integrated with SIEM and forensic tools; GPS-synchronised NTP with microsecond accuracy.

controls.title: DDoS Protection
controls.description: Implement DDoS protection measures including on-premise mitigation, cloud-based scrubbing services, and volumetric attack resilience for internet-facing services.

maturity.basic: Basic DDoS protection with rate limiting and traffic filtering; ISP-level DDoS mitigation engaged; incident response procedure for DDoS events.
maturity.mature: Hybrid DDoS protection combining on-premise appliances and cloud scrubbing; automated detection and mitigation; regular DDoS simulation tests.
maturity.advanced: Multi-layered DDoS protection with AI-based traffic analysis; automated traffic rerouting; anycast-based global traffic distribution; sub-second mitigation response.

controls.title: PII Encryption at Rest
controls.description: Encrypt all personally identifiable information (PII) and sensitive customer data at rest using industry-standard encryption algorithms and proper key management.

maturity.basic: AES-256 encryption applied to databases containing PII; encryption keys stored separately from encrypted data.
maturity.mature: Column-level encryption for PII fields; HSM-based key management; encryption coverage validated through automated scanning; key rotation schedules enforced.
maturity.advanced: Tokenisation for high-sensitivity PII; homomorphic encryption for analytics on encrypted data; automated PII discovery and encryption enforcement; format-preserving encryption.

controls.title: Data Loss Prevention Technology
controls.description: Deploy DLP technology to detect and prevent unauthorised transmission or exfiltration of sensitive data across all channels.

maturity.basic: DLP solution deployed monitoring email and web channels; basic content inspection rules for PII patterns.
maturity.mature: DLP coverage extended to endpoints, cloud services, and removable media; machine learning-based content classification; incident workflow with automated notifications.
maturity.advanced: Unified DLP platform across all channels with user behaviour analytics; automated policy tuning based on false positive analysis; integration with CASB for cloud DLP.

controls.title: DLP Policy Coverage — In-Use, In-Motion, At-Rest
controls.description: Ensure DLP policies comprehensively cover data in all three states: in-use on endpoints, in-motion across networks, and at-rest in storage systems.

maturity.basic: DLP policies defined for each data state; monitoring active for email attachments and USB transfers.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive coverage including clipboard monitoring, screen capture prevention, cloud uploads, and database access; policies aligned with data classification.
maturity.advanced: Context-aware DLP policies adapting to user role, location, and device; integration with identity governance; automated policy testing and validation.

evidenceRequired: DLP policy matrix by data state

controls.title: Secure Data Removal and Disposal
controls.description: Implement secure data removal and media disposal procedures ensuring complete destruction of sensitive data on decommissioned or repurposed assets.

maturity.basic: Data wiping procedures documented; certificates of destruction obtained for physical media; degaussing or shredding for magnetic media.
maturity.mature: Automated secure erasure integrated with asset decommissioning workflow; cryptographic erasure for encrypted storage; third-party destruction witnessed and certified.
maturity.advanced: Verified data destruction with audit trail in asset management system; automated compliance reporting; blockchain-based certificates of destruction.

controls.title: Data Classification Framework
controls.description: Establish and enforce a data classification framework with defined sensitivity levels, handling requirements, and labelling standards.

maturity.basic: Data classification policy with defined levels (Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted); manual labelling process established.
maturity.mature: Automated classification tools deployed; classification integrated with DLP and access controls; regular classification review and reclassification process.
maturity.advanced: AI-powered automatic data classification; dynamic labelling based on content analysis; classification enforcement embedded in data lifecycle management.

controls.title: Data Inventory and Mapping
controls.description: Maintain a comprehensive inventory of sensitive data assets including location, ownership, access permissions, and data flow mapping across systems.

maturity.basic: Data inventory maintained listing sensitive data repositories with owners and classification; updated annually.
maturity.mature: Data flow mapping across systems and third parties; automated data discovery tools deployed; data lineage tracking for critical datasets.
maturity.advanced: Real-time data asset inventory with automated discovery and classification; data governance platform with full lineage; privacy impact assessments automated.

controls.title: Third-Party Data Controls
controls.description: Implement controls governing the sharing, processing, and storage of sensitive data by third-party service providers and partners.

maturity.basic: Contractual data protection clauses included in third-party agreements; data sharing agreements in place; basic access controls for third-party access.
maturity.mature: Third-party data handling assessed through security questionnaires and audits; DLP extended to third-party data exchanges; right-to-audit clauses exercised annually.
maturity.advanced: Continuous monitoring of third-party data handling; secure data rooms for controlled sharing; automated data access revocation upon contract termination.

controls.title: Confidential Information Barrier Prevention
controls.description: Implement controls to prevent confidential information barriers (Chinese walls) from being breached through logical access controls, monitoring, and segregation.

maturity.basic: Logical access controls enforce information barriers between business units; barrier policy documented and communicated.
maturity.mature: Automated monitoring of cross-barrier access attempts; regular access certification reviews; DLP rules aligned with barrier requirements.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic information barriers adapting to deal activity; real-time breach detection with automated containment; integrated with compliance surveillance systems.

controls.title: SOC Capability Establishment
controls.description: Establish a Security Operations Centre with defined mandate, processes, technology stack, and staffing to monitor, detect, and respond to security events.

maturity.basic: SOC established with defined operational hours; core monitoring tools deployed; L1/L2 analyst staffing in place.
maturity.mature: 24x7 SOC operations with L1/L2/L3 analysts; documented procedures and runbooks; service level objectives for detection and response times.
maturity.advanced: Advanced SOC with SOAR platform; automated tier-1 triage; threat hunting team; metrics-driven continuous improvement; SOC maturity assessment against industry frameworks.

controls.title: SIEM Deployment and Management
controls.description: Deploy and maintain a Security Information and Event Management platform with comprehensive log ingestion, correlation rules, and alerting.

maturity.basic: SIEM deployed with log sources from critical systems; basic correlation rules and alerting configured; log retention meeting regulatory requirements.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive log coverage including cloud, endpoint, and application layers; custom correlation rules aligned with MITRE ATT&CK; regular rule tuning cycles.
maturity.advanced: ML-augmented SIEM with UEBA; automated playbook execution via SOAR integration; advanced analytics and threat scoring; real-time compliance monitoring dashboards.

controls.title: Incident Coordination and Triage
controls.description: Establish SOC-driven incident coordination processes including triage, classification, assignment, and tracking of security incidents through resolution.

maturity.basic: Incident triage process documented; severity classification scheme defined; ticketing system for incident tracking.
maturity.mature: Automated initial triage for common incident types; SLA-based response times by severity; incident coordinator role defined with handoff procedures.
maturity.advanced: AI-assisted triage with automated enrichment; orchestrated response workflows; real-time incident dashboards; integration with external threat intelligence for context.

controls.title: Vulnerability Management Programme
controls.description: Maintain a continuous vulnerability management programme integrated with SOC operations for prioritisation, tracking, and remediation of discovered vulnerabilities.

maturity.basic: Regular vulnerability scanning of infrastructure; findings tracked in vulnerability management system; remediation SLAs defined by severity.
maturity.mature: Continuous scanning with asset criticality-based prioritisation; integration with patch management; exception and compensating control tracking.
maturity.advanced: Risk-based vulnerability management correlating vulnerability data with threat intelligence and asset criticality; predictive vulnerability analysis; automated remediation for standard fixes.

controls.title: Proactive Threat Hunting
controls.description: Conduct proactive threat hunting operations to identify advanced threats and indicators of compromise that evade automated detection controls.

maturity.basic: Periodic threat hunting campaigns conducted based on published threat advisories and IoCs; documented hunting procedures.
maturity.mature: Regular hypothesis-driven threat hunting using MITRE ATT&CK framework; dedicated hunting team; findings feed back into SIEM detection rules.
maturity.advanced: Continuous threat hunting with custom tooling and data science techniques; proactive adversary emulation; hunting outcomes drive strategic security investments.

controls.title: Digital Forensic Capability
controls.description: Maintain digital forensic investigation capability with trained personnel, appropriate tools, evidence handling procedures, and chain-of-custody protocols.

maturity.basic: Forensic tools available (disk imaging, memory analysis); at least one trained forensic analyst; evidence handling procedures documented.
maturity.mature: Dedicated forensic lab with validated tools; certified forensic analysts (EnCE, GCFE); chain-of-custody processes tested; remote forensic collection capability.
maturity.advanced: Advanced forensic capabilities including malware reverse engineering, memory forensics, and mobile forensics; automated evidence collection and preservation; forensic readiness programme.

controls.title: Threat Intelligence and IoC Management
controls.description: Subscribe to and operationalise threat intelligence feeds, managing indicators of compromise for proactive detection and prevention.

maturity.basic: Subscribed to industry threat intelligence feeds (FS-ISAC, government CERTs); manual IoC ingestion into security tools.
maturity.mature: Automated IoC ingestion via STIX/TAXII; threat intelligence platform (TIP) deployed; contextualised intelligence briefings produced for stakeholders.
maturity.advanced: Multi-source intelligence fusion with automated correlation; custom intelligence production; strategic, tactical, and operational intelligence products; intelligence-driven defence.

controls.title: SOC Playbook Library
controls.description: Develop and maintain a library of SOC response playbooks covering common attack scenarios, ensuring consistent and effective incident handling.

maturity.basic: Playbooks documented for top 10 incident types (malware, phishing, unauthorised access, DDoS, etc.); annual review cycle.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive playbook library covering MITRE ATT&CK techniques; playbooks tested through tabletop exercises; semi-annual updates based on lessons learned.
maturity.advanced: SOAR-integrated automated playbooks; continuous testing through purple team exercises; adaptive playbooks that update based on threat intelligence; metrics on playbook effectiveness.

controls.title: Monthly SOC Reporting
controls.description: Produce monthly SOC operational reports covering detection metrics, incident statistics, threat trends, and improvement recommendations for management review.

maturity.basic: Monthly reports produced covering incident volumes, types, and resolution times; distributed to IT management.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive monthly reports with trend analysis, threat landscape updates, KPI/KRI tracking; presented to senior management with recommendations.
maturity.advanced: Real-time SOC dashboards supplementing monthly reports; predictive trend analysis; board-level executive summaries; benchmarking against industry peers.

controls.title: SOC Physical Security and Holistic View
controls.description: Ensure SOC facilities have adequate physical security controls and the SOC maintains a holistic view across all technology environments including cloud and third-party.

maturity.basic: SOC in physically secured area with access controls; monitoring covers on-premise infrastructure; basic visibility into cloud environments.
maturity.mature: Dedicated SOC facility with biometric access; comprehensive visibility across on-premise, cloud, and hybrid environments; integrated monitoring dashboards.
maturity.advanced: Hardened SOC facility with TEMPEST considerations; unified visibility across all environments including OT/IoT; global SOC follow-the-sun model; SOC redundancy.

controls.title: VAPT Standard Operating Procedure
controls.description: Establish and maintain comprehensive VAPT SOPs covering scope, methodology, tools, reporting standards, and remediation tracking processes.

maturity.basic: VAPT SOP documented covering scope definition, methodology selection, and reporting templates; approved by CISO.
maturity.mature: SOP includes risk-based scoping, multiple testing methodologies (OWASP, PTES, OSSTMM), quality assurance process; reviewed annually.
maturity.advanced: SOP continuously updated with emerging attack techniques; integrated with DevSecOps pipeline; automated quality scoring of test outputs.

controls.title: Quarterly Vulnerability Assessment
controls.description: Conduct quarterly vulnerability assessments of all in-scope infrastructure, applications, and services with comprehensive coverage and timely remediation.

maturity.basic: Quarterly VA conducted on critical infrastructure; authenticated scanning; findings reported and tracked for remediation.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive quarterly VA covering all infrastructure, applications, and cloud assets; risk-prioritised remediation with SLA tracking; re-validation scans.
maturity.advanced: Continuous VA integrated with asset management; automated remediation for common findings; predictive vulnerability analysis; zero-day exposure assessment.

controls.title: Annual Penetration Testing
controls.description: Conduct annual penetration testing of critical systems and applications using qualified external testers with comprehensive scope and realistic attack simulation.

maturity.basic: Annual pen test conducted by qualified external firm; scope covers critical internet-facing and internal systems; executive summary and detailed findings report.
maturity.mature: Annual pen test with expanded scope including social engineering, wireless, and physical; CREST/OSCP-qualified testers; remediation verified through retest.
maturity.advanced: Multiple pen tests annually with objective-based testing (CBEST/TIBER approach); assumed breach scenarios; red team engagement with purple team follow-up.

controls.title: Pre-Launch Penetration Testing
controls.description: Conduct penetration testing on all new applications and significant changes before production deployment to identify and remediate security vulnerabilities.

maturity.basic: Pre-launch pen test mandatory for new internet-facing applications; gate in deployment process requiring pen test sign-off.
maturity.mature: Pre-launch pen test for all significant changes; integrated with change management process; risk-based scoping criteria; fast-track process for urgent changes.
maturity.advanced: Automated security testing in CI/CD pipeline supplementing manual pen tests; DAST/IAST integrated into deployment; security champion sign-off required.

controls.title: Penetration Test Documentation and Escalation
controls.description: Ensure comprehensive documentation of penetration test findings with appropriate escalation of critical vulnerabilities to senior management and timely remediation.

maturity.basic: Pen test findings documented with severity ratings; critical findings escalated to CISO within 24 hours; remediation timelines assigned.
maturity.mature: Detailed technical and executive reporting; trend analysis across tests; escalation to risk committee for systemic issues; formal risk acceptance for unresolved findings.
maturity.advanced: Automated finding correlation with vulnerability management; predictive trend analysis; real-time escalation dashboards; independent validation of remediation.

controls.title: Compromise Assessment
controls.description: Conduct periodic compromise assessments to identify evidence of past or ongoing breaches, advanced threats, or hidden adversary presence in the environment.

maturity.basic: Annual compromise assessment conducted by qualified third party; scope covers critical systems and networks.
maturity.mature: Semi-annual compromise assessments with expanded scope; combination of network traffic analysis, endpoint forensics, and log analysis; findings integrated with incident response.
maturity.advanced: Continuous compromise assessment capability using automated threat detection; dark web monitoring for compromised credentials; adversary simulation to validate detection.

controls.title: Crowdsourced Security Testing
controls.description: Implement a crowdsourced security testing or bug bounty programme to leverage external researchers for identifying vulnerabilities in public-facing systems.

maturity.basic: Vulnerability disclosure policy published; responsible disclosure process established; dedicated channel for external vulnerability reports.
maturity.mature: Managed bug bounty programme through reputable platform; defined scope, reward structure, and triage process; integration with vulnerability management.
maturity.advanced: Continuous bug bounty with expanded scope; VIP researcher programme; live hacking events; vulnerability trend analysis informing security strategy.

controls.title: API Inventory and Classification
controls.description: Maintain a comprehensive inventory of all APIs including internal, external, and third-party APIs with classification by sensitivity and criticality.

maturity.basic: API inventory maintained listing all production APIs with owners, endpoints, and data sensitivity classification.
maturity.mature: Automated API discovery tools deployed; APIs classified by risk level; shadow API detection; inventory updated through CI/CD pipeline integration.
maturity.advanced: Real-time API inventory with automated classification; API behaviour baseline tracking; automated compliance verification; full API lifecycle governance.

controls.title: API Availability and Scaling
controls.description: Ensure API infrastructure supports required availability levels with appropriate scaling, load balancing, and resilience measures.

maturity.basic: API availability targets defined; basic load balancing implemented; capacity planning conducted annually.
maturity.mature: Auto-scaling configured for APIs; rate limiting and throttling implemented; API performance monitoring with alerting; DR provisions for critical APIs.
maturity.advanced: Geo-distributed API infrastructure; intelligent traffic management; circuit breaker patterns implemented; chaos engineering for API resilience validation.

controls.title: API Secure Coding Practices
controls.description: Enforce secure coding practices for API development including input validation, output encoding, error handling, and protection against OWASP API Top 10 risks.

maturity.basic: Secure API coding guidelines documented; code review includes security checks; basic input validation enforced.
maturity.mature: SAST and DAST tools integrated into API development pipeline; security unit tests mandatory; OWASP API Top 10 addressed in guidelines; developer training programme.
maturity.advanced: Automated security gate in CI/CD pipeline; AI-assisted code review for security; API specification-driven security testing; secure-by-design API templates.

controls.title: API Encryption and Key Management
controls.description: Implement strong encryption for all API communications and establish robust key management practices for API authentication credentials and encryption keys.

maturity.basic: TLS 1.2+ enforced for all API communications; API keys managed through centralised key management; keys rotated periodically.
maturity.mature: mTLS for internal API communications; HSM-based key management; automated key rotation; certificate lifecycle management; payload encryption for sensitive data.
maturity.advanced: Automated certificate and key management; short-lived credentials; API-specific encryption policies; quantum-resistant algorithm readiness assessment.

controls.title: API Anti-Brute-Force Controls
controls.description: Implement anti-brute-force measures for API authentication including rate limiting, account lockout, CAPTCHA, and anomaly detection.

maturity.basic: Rate limiting configured on authentication endpoints; account lockout after failed attempts; logging of failed authentication events.
maturity.mature: Adaptive rate limiting based on risk signals; progressive delays; IP-based and account-based throttling; real-time alerting on brute force patterns.
maturity.advanced: ML-based credential stuffing detection; behavioural biometrics; automated IP reputation blocking; device fingerprinting for authentication context.

controls.title: API Authentication and Authorisation Protocols
controls.description: Implement robust authentication and authorisation protocols for APIs including OAuth 2.0, OpenID Connect, and fine-grained access controls.

maturity.basic: OAuth 2.0 implemented for API authentication; role-based access controls; API key management for service-to-service communication.
maturity.mature: OpenID Connect with JWT tokens; scope-based authorisation; centralised API identity management; regular access reviews; token expiry policies enforced.
maturity.advanced: Fine-grained attribute-based access control (ABAC); dynamic authorisation based on context; decentralised identity integration; continuous authentication for long-lived sessions.

controls.title: API Gateway Deployment
controls.description: Deploy API gateway infrastructure for centralised policy enforcement, traffic management, monitoring, and security control for all API communications.

maturity.basic: API gateway deployed for external-facing APIs; centralised authentication and rate limiting; basic request/response logging.
maturity.mature: API gateway for all API traffic (internal and external); WAF integration; request validation and schema enforcement; versioning management.
maturity.advanced: Multi-gateway architecture with service mesh; automated policy deployment; real-time threat detection at gateway; API traffic analytics and anomaly detection.

controls.title: Periodic API Security Assessments
controls.description: Conduct periodic security assessments of APIs including penetration testing, configuration reviews, and compliance checks against API security standards.

maturity.basic: Annual API penetration testing; configuration review of API gateway and infrastructure; findings tracked for remediation.
maturity.mature: Quarterly API security assessments; automated API fuzzing; business logic testing; assessment scope covers internal and third-party API integrations.
maturity.advanced: Continuous API security testing integrated with CI/CD; runtime API protection testing; adversarial testing of API abuse scenarios; API threat modelling.

controls.title: Continuous API Monitoring
controls.description: Implement continuous monitoring of API traffic, behaviour, and performance to detect anomalies, abuse patterns, and potential security incidents.

maturity.basic: API access logging enabled; basic monitoring dashboards; alerting on error rates and response time anomalies.
maturity.mature: API behaviour analytics tracking usage patterns; automated anomaly detection; integration with SIEM; API abuse detection rules.
maturity.advanced: ML-driven API traffic analysis; real-time API threat detection; automated response to API abuse; predictive analytics for capacity and security.

evidenceRequired: Incident response records from API events

controls.title: API Token Revocation and Session Management
controls.description: Implement robust API token revocation mechanisms and session management controls including token lifecycle management and emergency revocation capabilities.

maturity.basic: Token revocation endpoints implemented; token expiry enforced; session management for stateful APIs documented.
maturity.mature: Centralised token revocation service; short-lived access tokens with refresh token rotation; real-time token blacklisting; automated revocation on anomaly detection.
maturity.advanced: Distributed token revocation with near-zero propagation delay; automated mass revocation capability for incident response; proof-of-possession tokens; token binding.

controls.title: Cyber Attack Lifecycle Defence
controls.description: Implement defence capabilities mapped to each stage of the cyber attack lifecycle (reconnaissance through actions on objectives) to detect and disrupt attacks at every phase.

maturity.basic: Defence controls mapped to kill chain phases; detection capabilities at perimeter, network, and endpoint layers.
maturity.mature: Layered defence validated against MITRE ATT&CK framework; detection coverage metrics tracked; gaps identified and prioritised for remediation.
maturity.advanced: Continuous ATT&CK coverage assessment; automated adversary emulation testing defences; deception technology deployed; adaptive defence that evolves with threat actor TTPs.

controls.title: Continuous Security Monitoring
controls.description: Implement continuous monitoring across all technology assets and environments to maintain real-time awareness of security posture and emerging threats.

maturity.basic: Monitoring covers critical infrastructure and internet-facing systems; defined monitoring use cases; alert response procedures documented.
maturity.mature: Comprehensive monitoring across on-premise, cloud, and hybrid environments; user behaviour monitoring; automated baseline establishment and deviation detection.
maturity.advanced: Unified monitoring platform with full-stack visibility; AI-driven anomaly detection; predictive threat analytics; automated response orchestration; continuous compliance monitoring.

controls.title: Cyber Threat Intelligence Programme
controls.description: Establish a structured cyber threat intelligence programme producing actionable intelligence that informs defensive operations and strategic security decisions.

maturity.basic: Consumption of external threat intelligence feeds; basic IOC matching; intelligence shared with SOC team.
maturity.mature: Dedicated CTI function producing tactical, operational, and strategic intelligence; intelligence requirements defined by stakeholders; threat landscape reports published quarterly.
maturity.advanced: Intelligence-driven security programme; threat actor profiling and tracking; predictive intelligence capabilities; active participation in intelligence sharing communities; original intelligence production.

controls.title: Anomalous Activity Detection and Response
controls.description: Implement capabilities to detect anomalous activities across networks, systems, and user behaviours with defined response procedures for confirmed anomalies.

maturity.basic: Baseline behaviours established for critical systems; anomaly detection rules in SIEM; response procedures for confirmed anomalies.
maturity.mature: UEBA deployed with behavioural baselines for users and entities; automated alerting on deviations; contextual enrichment for analyst investigation.
maturity.advanced: ML-driven multi-dimensional anomaly detection; graph analytics for complex attack pattern detection; automated containment for high-confidence anomalies; continuous baseline adaptation.

controls.title: Red Team Operations
controls.description: Conduct red team exercises simulating realistic adversary tactics, techniques, and procedures to validate the effectiveness of detective and preventive controls.

maturity.basic: Annual red team exercise conducted by qualified external team; scope covers critical attack paths; findings reported with remediation recommendations.
maturity.mature: Semi-annual red team engagements with varied scenarios; purple team follow-up to validate improvements; red team findings drive security investment priorities.
maturity.advanced: Continuous red team programme with persistent adversary simulation; TIBER/CBEST-aligned exercises; automated adversary emulation supplementing manual red team; red team as a service internally.

controls.title: Cyber Crisis Management Framework
controls.description: Establish a cyber crisis management framework defining governance structures, decision-making authorities, communication protocols, and escalation procedures for major cyber incidents.

maturity.basic: Crisis management framework documented; crisis team composition defined with contact information; basic escalation triggers established.
maturity.mature: Crisis management team trained and exercised regularly; pre-defined decision trees for common crisis scenarios; internal and external communication templates prepared.
maturity.advanced: Crisis management framework tested through sophisticated simulations; integration with business continuity and disaster recovery; automated crisis notification and assembly; crisis analytics capability.

controls.title: Cyber Incident Response Plan
controls.description: Establish and maintain a comprehensive CIRP covering detection, containment, eradication, recovery, and post-incident activities with defined roles and responsibilities.

maturity.basic: CIRP documented with phases, roles, and responsibilities; incident classification scheme; contact list maintained; reviewed annually.
maturity.mature: CIRP tested through regular exercises; integrated with SOC procedures; automated workflow for common incident types; lessons learned systematically captured.
maturity.advanced: CIRP continuously updated based on threat intelligence; automated playbook execution; integration with external CERT and law enforcement; metrics-driven continuous improvement.

controls.title: CERT Readiness
controls.description: Ensure Computer Emergency Response Team readiness with trained personnel, established procedures, tooling, and coordination protocols with external CERTs.

maturity.basic: Internal CERT established with defined roles; coordination procedures with MyCERT and sector CERT documented; incident handling tools available.
maturity.mature: CERT members hold relevant certifications (GCIH, ECIH); regular training and exercises; established relationships with national and sector CERTs; 24x7 on-call rotation.
maturity.advanced: CERT operates at international standards; participates in FIRST or APCERT; advanced forensic and malware analysis capabilities; contributes to threat intelligence sharing.

controls.title: Out-of-Band Communications
controls.description: Establish and maintain out-of-band communication channels for use during cyber incidents when primary communication systems may be compromised.

maturity.basic: Alternative communication channels identified (satellite phones, personal mobile numbers, encrypted messaging); contact lists maintained offline.
maturity.mature: Out-of-band communication channels tested quarterly; multiple redundant channels available; pre-configured secure communication platform for crisis team.
maturity.advanced: Encrypted out-of-band communication platform with automated activation during incidents; tested under realistic scenarios; hardware-based secure communication for executive team.

controls.title: Annual Cyber Drill
controls.description: Conduct at least one comprehensive annual cyber drill simulating realistic cyber attack scenarios to test the organisation's response capabilities.

maturity.basic: Annual cyber drill conducted with defined scenario; participation from IT security and key stakeholders; after-action report produced.
maturity.mature: Annual drill with multi-phase scenarios involving business units, senior management, and communications team; independent observers; improvement actions tracked.
maturity.advanced: Multiple drills annually including surprise exercises; cross-organisational drills with regulators and industry peers; live-fire exercises with real attack simulation; board participation.

controls.title: Drill Scenario Realism and Coverage
controls.description: Ensure cyber drill scenarios are realistic, cover diverse threat types, and include current threat landscape elements to adequately test response capabilities.

maturity.basic: Drill scenarios based on common threat types (ransomware, data breach, DDoS); scenarios reviewed for relevance before execution.
maturity.mature: Scenarios developed with threat intelligence input; cover emerging threats; vary between tabletop, functional, and full-scale exercises; third-party scenario design.
maturity.advanced: Scenarios based on threat actor profiling specific to the institution; multi-vector combined scenarios; scenarios include supply chain and third-party compromise; lessons from real incidents incorporated.

controls.title: Drill Board Reporting
controls.description: Report cyber drill outcomes and findings to the board of directors including identified gaps, improvement recommendations, and remediation timelines.

maturity.basic: Board briefed on annual drill results; summary of findings and recommendations presented.
maturity.mature: Detailed board report with gap analysis, trend comparison against previous drills, and prioritised remediation roadmap; board discussion documented.
maturity.advanced: Board receives comprehensive resilience assessment linking drill outcomes to risk appetite; benchmarking against industry peers; board tracks remediation progress quarterly.

controls.title: Post-Incident Review
controls.description: Conduct thorough post-incident reviews for all significant cyber incidents to identify root causes, lessons learned, and improvement actions.

maturity.basic: Post-incident review conducted for all major incidents; root cause analysis documented; lessons learned shared with relevant teams.
maturity.mature: Structured post-incident review process with timeline reconstruction, blameless retrospective, and tracked improvement actions; trend analysis across incidents.
maturity.advanced: Automated incident timeline reconstruction; cross-incident pattern analysis; lessons learned feed into training, detection rules, and architecture improvements; industry incident learning programme.

controls.title: Cyber Insurance Coverage
controls.description: Obtain and maintain adequate cyber insurance coverage commensurate with the institution's cyber risk profile, potential loss exposure, and regulatory expectations.

maturity.basic: Cyber insurance policy obtained; coverage scope documented; policy reviewed at renewal; basic coverage for data breach and business interruption.
maturity.mature: Coverage aligned with quantified cyber risk exposure; comprehensive policy covering first and third-party losses; coverage adequacy assessed annually against evolving threats.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic insurance programme adapted to real-time risk posture; parametric cyber insurance for rapid payout; coverage stress-tested against catastrophic scenarios; war exclusion analysis.

controls.title: Insurance Terms and Conditions Review
controls.description: Regularly review cyber insurance terms, conditions, exclusions, and sub-limits to ensure they align with the institution's risk profile and regulatory requirements.

maturity.basic: Policy T&C reviewed at each renewal; exclusions documented and understood by risk management team.
maturity.mature: Independent legal review of policy terms; gap analysis between coverage and risk exposure; scenario testing of claims against policy terms.
maturity.advanced: Continuous monitoring of coverage adequacy; policy terms benchmarked against industry best practice; proactive negotiation of coverage improvements; participation in industry insurance forums.

controls.title: Insurance Policyholder Obligations
controls.description: Ensure compliance with all cyber insurance policyholder obligations including security control requirements, notification obligations, and claims procedures.

maturity.basic: Policyholder obligations documented and assigned to responsible parties; notification requirements understood; claims procedure documented.
maturity.mature: Obligations tracked through compliance management system; regular self-assessment against policy requirements; pre-incident claims preparation procedures.
maturity.advanced: Automated compliance monitoring against policy obligations; real-time reporting capability for insurers; integrated claims management with incident response.

controls.title: Cyber Incident Notification
controls.description: Establish and follow procedures for timely notification of cyber incidents to BNM, law enforcement, and other stakeholders as required by regulations.

maturity.basic: Notification procedures documented with defined timelines for BNM reporting; notification templates prepared; responsible officer designated.
maturity.mature: Automated notification triggers based on incident severity; multi-stakeholder notification workflow; legal review integrated into notification process; practice runs conducted.
maturity.advanced: Real-time incident reporting capability; automated regulatory report generation; integrated stakeholder communication platform; post-notification follow-up tracking.

controls.title: Threat Intelligence Sharing and Stakeholder Collaboration
controls.description: Participate in industry threat intelligence sharing initiatives and collaborate with stakeholders including regulators, CERTs, and peer institutions on cyber threats.

maturity.basic: Participation in BNM/industry threat intelligence sharing forums; basic IoC sharing with sector peers; subscription to government threat advisories.
maturity.mature: Active contribution to threat intelligence sharing platforms (STIX/TAXII); bi-directional sharing with FS-ISAC or equivalent; regular engagement with NACSA and MyCERT.
maturity.advanced: Leadership role in industry cyber collaboration; automated real-time threat sharing; joint threat analysis with sector peers; cross-border intelligence sharing; contribution to policy development.

controls.title: CRF Expansion for Digital Services
controls.description: Extend the Cyber Resilience Framework to comprehensively cover digital service delivery channels including mobile banking, internet banking, and digital payment services.

maturity.basic: CRF scope explicitly includes digital service channels; risks specific to digital services identified and assessed.
maturity.mature: Dedicated digital service risk assessment methodology; CRF controls tailored for each digital channel; regular risk reassessment as services evolve.
maturity.advanced: Dynamic CRF adapting to new digital services in real-time; automated risk assessment for new digital features; integrated digital risk management across customer journey.

controls.title: Alternative Controls Documentation
controls.description: Document and justify alternative controls where standard security controls cannot be applied to digital services, ensuring equivalent protection levels.

maturity.basic: Alternative controls documented with justification; risk acceptance for residual risk; periodic review of alternative controls.
maturity.mature: Formal alternative control assessment process with independent validation; compensating controls effectiveness measured; regular reassessment for standard control applicability.
maturity.advanced: Continuous assessment of alternative control effectiveness; automated compensating control monitoring; proactive identification of opportunities to replace with standard controls.

controls.title: Fraud Countermeasure Coverage
controls.description: Implement comprehensive fraud countermeasures across all digital service channels covering transaction fraud, account takeover, identity theft, and social engineering.

maturity.basic: Basic fraud detection rules for digital transactions; transaction limits and velocity checks; SMS/email notifications for transactions.
maturity.mature: Multi-layered fraud detection with rule-based and ML models; real-time transaction scoring; device fingerprinting; behavioural analytics for account takeover detection.
maturity.advanced: AI-driven fraud detection with real-time decisioning; network analysis for fraud ring detection; predictive fraud modelling; adaptive authentication based on fraud risk score.

controls.title: Defence-in-Depth for Mobile Services
controls.description: Implement defence-in-depth security architecture for mobile banking and payment applications including app hardening, runtime protection, and secure communication.

maturity.basic: Mobile app security baseline including certificate pinning, code obfuscation, root/jailbreak detection; secure communication channels.
maturity.mature: RASP (Runtime Application Self-Protection) integrated; mobile threat defence; secure local storage; biometric authentication; app integrity verification.
maturity.advanced: Advanced mobile app shielding; real-time threat detection within app; dynamic security responses; continuous mobile threat intelligence; secure element integration.

controls.title: Continuous Digital Service Surveillance
controls.description: Implement continuous surveillance of digital service channels for security anomalies, fraud patterns, and service integrity issues.

maturity.basic: Transaction monitoring for digital channels; basic anomaly alerting; daily review of security events.
maturity.mature: Real-time monitoring across all digital channels; correlated analysis of customer behaviour; automated anomaly detection with risk scoring; 24x7 monitoring.
maturity.advanced: AI-powered continuous surveillance with predictive analytics; cross-channel correlation; real-time customer risk profiling; automated protective actions.

controls.title: Digital Incident Handling and Coordinated Response
controls.description: Establish specialised incident handling procedures for digital service security incidents ensuring coordinated response across IT, fraud, operations, and customer service teams.

maturity.basic: Digital service incident procedures documented; coordination between IT security, fraud team, and customer service defined; basic communication templates.
maturity.mature: Integrated incident response for digital channels with automated workflows; real-time coordination platform; customer notification procedures; regulatory reporting integration.
maturity.advanced: Orchestrated response across all teams with automated containment; customer impact assessment automated; proactive customer communication; integration with fraud management systems.

controls.title: Senior Management and Board Reporting on Digital Security
controls.description: Provide regular reporting to senior management and board on digital service security posture, fraud trends, incident statistics, and improvement initiatives.

maturity.basic: Quarterly reports on digital service security metrics and fraud statistics provided to senior management.
maturity.mature: Monthly detailed reporting with trend analysis, peer benchmarking, and risk-adjusted metrics; board receives quarterly digital risk dashboards.
maturity.advanced: Real-time digital security dashboards for management; predictive analytics on fraud trends; automated report generation; board-level KRI tracking with thresholds.

controls.title: Fraud Detection System Updates
controls.description: Regularly update fraud detection systems with new rules, patterns, and models based on emerging fraud typologies and threat intelligence.

maturity.basic: Fraud detection rules updated quarterly based on known fraud patterns; post-fraud analysis informs rule updates.
maturity.mature: Monthly fraud model updates; ML model retraining with recent fraud data; integration with industry fraud intelligence; A/B testing of new detection rules.
maturity.advanced: Continuous model updates with real-time learning; automated rule generation from fraud pattern analysis; cross-industry fraud intelligence sharing; adversarial ML defence.

controls.title: Secure Customer Communication
controls.description: Implement secure communication channels with customers for digital services including authenticated messaging, secure notifications, and anti-phishing measures.

maturity.basic: Secure in-app messaging for sensitive communications; consistent branding to help customers identify legitimate communications; anti-phishing guidance provided.
maturity.mature: Digitally signed communications; branded SMS sender IDs; domain-based message authentication (DMARC); proactive phishing domain takedown service.
maturity.advanced: Real-time phishing detection and takedown; customer communication verification platform; AI-powered impersonation detection; secure video communication for high-value interactions.

controls.title: Customer Empowerment and Kill Switch
controls.description: Provide customers with security controls including transaction limits management, device management, notification preferences, and emergency kill switch to disable digital services.

maturity.basic: Customers can set transaction limits; basic notification settings available; emergency contact number for service suspension.
maturity.mature: Self-service security dashboard for customers; device management and deregistration; real-time transaction notifications; one-click service suspension (kill switch) in app.
maturity.advanced: Granular customer security controls; AI-powered personalised security recommendations; automated protective actions based on risk; instant kill switch with automated fraud investigation trigger.

controls.title: Post-Incident Customer Measures
controls.description: Implement post-incident customer protection measures including account recovery, fraud reimbursement processes, credit monitoring, and customer communication protocols.

maturity.basic: Customer incident notification procedures; account recovery process documented; fraud reimbursement policy established; customer support trained on incident handling.
maturity.mature: Proactive customer notification with personalised guidance; expedited account recovery; automated fraud reimbursement for qualifying incidents; dedicated incident support team.
maturity.advanced: Automated post-incident customer protection activation; predictive identification of at-risk customers; comprehensive victim support programme; continuous monitoring of affected accounts.

controls.title: Staff Cybersecurity Awareness Programme
controls.description: Implement a comprehensive cybersecurity awareness programme for all staff covering phishing, social engineering, data handling, password hygiene, and incident reporting.

maturity.basic: Annual cybersecurity awareness training mandatory for all staff; basic phishing awareness content; completion tracking in LMS.
maturity.mature: Quarterly awareness campaigns with varied content; simulated phishing exercises with targeted training for failures; role-based awareness modules; awareness metrics tracked.
maturity.advanced: Continuous awareness programme with gamification; adaptive learning paths based on risk profile; behavioural change measurement; security champion programme in business units.

controls.title: Technical Staff Specialised Training
controls.description: Provide specialised cybersecurity training for technical staff including secure coding, security operations, incident response, and relevant certifications.

maturity.basic: Annual technical training plan for IT security staff; access to online training platforms; at least one certification per team member.
maturity.mature: Structured training roadmap aligned with career development; hands-on training (CTF, labs); conference attendance; cross-training between security disciplines.
maturity.advanced: Continuous professional development with advanced certifications (OSCP, GXPN, SANS); internal knowledge sharing programme; external speaking and contribution; training effectiveness validated through exercises.

controls.title: Board Cybersecurity Training
controls.description: Provide board of directors with regular cybersecurity education to ensure informed oversight of cyber risk management and strategic decision-making.

maturity.basic: Annual board cybersecurity briefing covering threat landscape, institutional risk posture, and regulatory developments.
maturity.mature: Semi-annual board education with external expert presentations; board tabletop exercises; tailored content for board members' roles on risk committee.
maturity.advanced: Continuous board education programme; board cyber competency assessment; peer benchmarking; board members participate in industry cyber forums; scenario-based strategic exercises.

controls.title: Third-Party Security Awareness
controls.description: Extend cybersecurity awareness requirements to third-party service providers, contractors, and partners with access to institutional systems or data.

maturity.basic: Third-party personnel required to complete basic security awareness training before system access; acknowledgement of security policies.
maturity.mature: Tailored awareness programme for third parties based on access level; inclusion in phishing simulations; annual refresher training; compliance tracking.
maturity.advanced: Integrated third-party awareness with continuous assessment; security scorecard for third-party awareness compliance; joint exercises with critical third parties; contractual awareness KPIs.
